mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 4.5% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in real personal savings rates indicate a demand-supply mismatch that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between the joint INDPRO-RSAFS composite and forward SPX returns remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment, rising housing starts act as a leading indicator of consumer credit expansion, which boosts near-term corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant difference between periods where HOUST rises and FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% versus baseline (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate demand, which directly supports earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward SPX returns are uncorrelated with quarterly GDPC1 growth rates over a 5-year rolling window (correlation coefficient < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 245.0, it signals entrenched inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward predictive accuracy drops below 50% when CPIAUCSL breaches the 245.0 threshold, indicating no directional edge.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate combined with declining real personal savings rates indicates a structural shift in consumer spending power, which reduces corporate revenue growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint interaction term of UNRATE and RSAFS fails to explain more than 2% of the variance in 20-day forward SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight monetary policy, an increase in industrial production signals resilient supply chains that outpace demand destruction, supporting corporate margins and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: tight monetary policy with supply resilience
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative or flat when INDPRO rises alongside FEDFUNDS above 3.5%, contradicting the supply-resilience premium hypothesis.
